title: Clinical Variant Extract — Sarah K. Sahl
subtitle: Source: 23andMe raw genotype file  ·  27 clinically characterized variants  ·  hg19 coordinates  ·  Research use only — not clinical diagnostic results

clinicalVariants.note: Arg389Gly. Beta-1 receptor variant affecting metoprolol response. Top 0.3% AlphaGenome signal in cardiac tissue.
Sinus node channel variant. Relevant to Inappropriate Sinus Tachycardia diagnosis.
VDR TaqI — affects vitamin D receptor function. Relevant to bone density and immune regulation.
Ala698Thr. vEDS-associated COL3A1 variant. Highest AlphaGenome signal in full batch (quantile 0.9995) in cardiac tissue. Pending formal genetics evaluation.
c.677C>T. Homozygous (GG) — reduced MTHFR enzyme activity. Affects folate/homocysteine metabolism. Combined with rs1801131, both MTHFR checkpoints affected.
c.1298A>C. Heterozygous — second MTHFR variant. Compounds homocysteine clearance risk.
IL-6 promoter variant. Modulates baseline IL-6 expression — relevant to chronic inflammatory state.
TNF-alpha promoter variant. Modulates TNF production. Relevant to systemic inflammation.
BDNF Val66Met. Affects BDNF secretion and neuroplasticity. Relevant to pain sensitization and psychiatric risk.
FKBP5 — PTSD stress response variant. Homozygous TT. Affects glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity and stress axis regulation.
FKBP5 — second PTSD variant. Homozygous CC. Compounds stress-axis dysregulation.
SLC6A4 3'UTR variant. Serotonin transporter. Relevant to fluoxetine response and serotonin system.
COMT Val158Met. Heterozygous AG = intermediate catecholamine clearance. Amplified pain signal transmission. Top 0.3% AlphaGenome signal in brain tissue.
CYP2C19 *2 allele. Homozygous AA = poor metabolizer. Reduced metabolism of fluoxetine and other CYP2C19 substrates. Drug accumulation risk.
CYP2C19 *3 allele. Homozygous GG = reference. No additional CYP2C19 *3 impact.
CYP2C19 *17 allele. Rapid metabolizer allele. Homozygous CC = reference (not rapid).
CYP2C9 *2 allele. Homozygous CC = reduced CYP2C9 function. Affects NSAID and warfarin metabolism.
CYP2C9 *3 allele. Homozygous AA = reference. No additional CYP2C9 *3 impact.
CYP2D6 *6 allele — non-functional allele. Affects metabolism of tramadol, atomoxetine, and other CYP2D6 substrates.
CYP2D6 *2 allele — missing data (--). May indicate gene deletion or copy number variant. CYP2D6 metabolizer status requires clinical testing.
OPRM1 A118G. Homozygous AA = altered opioid receptor binding affinity. May require higher opioid doses for equivalent analgesic effect.
SLCO1B1 *5 — statin myopathy risk. Homozygous TT = increased risk of statin-induced myopathy. Monitor for myalgia on rosuvastatin.
VKORC1 -1639G>A. Heterozygous CT — intermediate warfarin sensitivity. High AlphaGenome signal in muscle tissue (quantile 0.9995). Not currently on warfarin.
F5 Factor V Leiden. Homozygous CC = reference (no Leiden mutation). Factor V Leiden excluded.
ADRB2 Arg16Gly. Homozygous GG = reference for this variant. See rs1042714 for active ADRB2 signal.
ADRB2 Gln27Glu. Heterozygous CG — beta-2 receptor variant. Top 0.5% AlphaGenome signal in lung tissue. Affects albuterol and salmeterol target receptor.
GLCCI1 steroid resistance. Heterozygous CT — associated with reduced FEV1 improvement after ICS. Genomic correlate for documented steroid resistance.

pharmacogenomics.title: Pharmacogenomics Summary — Drug Metabolism & Response
pharmacogenomics.subtitle: Research-grade data from 23andMe. For clinical prescribing decisions, validated pharmacogenomic testing (e.g., GeneSight) is recommended.

rows.Clinical Implication: Significantly reduced CYP2C19 activity. Drug accumulation risk. Currently on fluoxetine — dose monitoring warranted.
Non-functional CYP2D6 allele. Tramadol may be less effective (reduced conversion to active form). Atomoxetine levels may be elevated.
Reduced CYP2C9 activity. NSAIDs are also contraindicated on cardiac/renal grounds — dual contraindication.
Increased statin myopathy risk. Currently on rosuvastatin 5mg. Monitor myalgia — note: baseline MSK pain from TTN/CTD may confound.
Intermediate catecholamine clearance → amplified pain signaling. Supports individualized analgesic dosing. Top 0.3% AlphaGenome signal in brain tissue.
Altered opioid receptor binding. May require higher doses for equivalent analgesic effect.
Beta-1 receptor variant. AlphaGenome quantile 0.9973 in cardiac tissue. Metoprolol titration target may respond atypically.
Beta-2 receptor variant. AlphaGenome quantile 0.9946 in lung tissue. Bronchodilator target receptor may have altered function.
Associated with reduced FEV1 improvement after ICS. Genomic correlate for documented steroid resistance pattern.
Intermediate warfarin sensitivity. Not currently on warfarin. Flag if anticoagulation is ever considered.
Homozygous — reduced MTHFR activity. Combined with rs1801131 heterozygous: both checkpoints affected. Monitor homocysteine; ensure adequate B12/folate.
